content.problemStatement: Chef likes to play table tennis. He found some statistics of matches which described who won the points in order. A game shall be won by the player first scoring 11 points except in the case when both players have 10 points each, then the game shall be won by the first player subsequently gaining a lead of 2 points. Could you please help the Chef find out who the winner was from the given statistics? (It is guaranteed that statistics represent  always a valid, finished match.)
constraints: The given sequence always corresponds to a finished match according to the official rules.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a binary string S (1 ≤ |S| ≤ 100). '1' denotes Chef winning a point, '0' denotes Chef losing a point. The sequence always represents a finished, valid table‑tennis game.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "WIN" if Chef won the match, otherwise "LOSE".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Chef scores 11 points while opponent scores 2 → Chef wins.\nTest case 2: Chef scores 3 points, opponent scores 11 → Chef loses.
Chef wins 11‑0, which satisfies the normal winning condition.
Both players reach 10‑10 after the first 20 points. Chef then wins two consecutive points, achieving a 12‑10 lead, so Chef wins.

solution.approach: Iterate over the characters of S while maintaining two counters: chefScore and opponentScore. After processing the whole string (the match is guaranteed to be finished) the winner is simply the player with the higher score. Because the input is always a valid finished game, no additional checks are required. The algorithm runs in linear time in the length of S and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, i.e. O(total length of all strings).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        s = data[idx]; idx += 1
        chef = s.count('1')
        opp = len(s) - chef
        out_lines.append('WIN' if chef > opp else 'LOSE')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
